dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - April 19, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**Nationals Shut Down Giants, 3-0**

A strong pitching performance from Andrew Alvarez led the Washington Nationals to a 3-0 shutout over the San Francisco Giants. Alvarez threw 4.1 innings, striking out five and allowing no earned runs. Curtis Mead's two-run homer in the top of the third gave the Nationals an early lead, and the team's bullpen closed out the game. Mead finished the day with a home run and two RBIs.

**Pirates Overcome Rays, 6-3**

The Pittsburgh Pirates rallied for a 6-3 win over the Tampa Bay Rays. Junior Caminero hit a solo homer in the bottom of the fifth to give the Pirates a 4-3 lead, and the team added two more runs in the seventh. Mitch Keller pitched seven strong innings, allowing two earned runs and striking out five.

**Yankees Cruise Past Royals, 7-0**

The New York Yankees dominated the Kansas City Royals, 7-0. Trent Grisham hit a three-run homer in the top of the fifth to give the Yankees a 5-0 lead, and the team's bullpen closed out the game. Ryan Weathers pitched 7.1 innings, striking out eight and allowing no earned runs.
